summary: The defining failure of the last three constellations cleared. The host-side commit stall that froze five of seven repos for six to nine days is gone: all six core repos committed today (2026-06-28), the committed-ready queue drained from 21 stranded items to 5 pending, and every 06-22 HIGH closed on disk. The distribution recovered from 1 healthy / 3 needs-attention / 1 at-risk / 1 stalled to 3 working / 3 in-progress / 0 broken. The portfolio's longest chronic finding, YaB's .finances remote-history exposure (eleven reviews), is fully retired. The 18 open actions across the boards are granular backlog detail (HBH 13, YaS 4, BR 1), not breakage. One process issue: the scheduled per-project tasks fired concurrently and committed their own 06-28 JSONs, the concurrent-unattended-session hazard.

topActions.label: Re-sequence the daily canonical-audit and dashboard-writer to run after the day's commits, not the morning slot; late-shipping repos (BR, YaS, YaA, YaB) carry stale workTreeClean/HEAD/metrics on the dashboard
Serialize or stagger the scheduled per-project bar-raise tasks against the weekly constellation; this run hit the concurrent-unattended-session hazard with both passes racing on the per-project JSONs
Push the un-pushed local-ahead commits on BR (2) and YaS (2) to origin so HEAD and origin/main reconcile
When gameplay drives the 3D crowd, migrate runners onto the crowd3d MultiMesh render path and re-baseline; the render-bound fps-cliff MED is at runsOpen 4, one run from chronic
Adopt YaC's Read-tool-confirms-whole discipline portfolio-wide so bash shell-read artifacts stop being misclassified as FUSE truncation
